page: Amazon Web Services m9g.16xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the ARMv9 architecture with Neoverse-V3 cores, it features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 34 Gbps. No local storage or GPUs are included.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 127950.05 and a Passmark physics score of 19065.9. Memory subsystem performance is strong, registering a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 9281.17 GB/sec and a Passmark memory score of 4594.11. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core scoring 2006.38. Data processing benchmarks are highly competitive, showing top-tier results in gzip compression at 36.42 GB/sec and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression at 888.56 GB/sec.

While the server excels in database operations with 58750.24 on Passmark and Redis SET operations at 183.27 million operations per second, it exhibits performance limitations in specific tasks. LLM text generation speeds are low, particularly for small models which register only 9.76 tokens per second. However, static web serving throughput is exceptional at 179.08 GB/sec. This hardware configuration is suited for multi-threaded enterprise applications, database hosting, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive caching layers, offering high resource density and architectural efficiency for ARM-native workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m9g.16xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server powered by 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, a 34 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor without local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 127950.05 and a cached memory read bandwidth of 9281.17 GB/sec. The server delivers high throughput for database operations and static web serving, though it exhibits lower performance in LLM text generation tasks. It is configured for multi-threaded enterprise applications, database hosting, and high-volume web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.16xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server with 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 34 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.16xlarge is an ARM64 server featuring 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core benchmark results.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose server featuring dedicated physical cores, high memory bandwidth, and exceptional multi-threaded data processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 physical cores on ARMv9 architecture
256 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and decompression benchmarks
34 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for database hosting, Redis caching, and static web serving
Lower performance efficiency in LLM text generation tasks
